summary: Despite the lingual nerve being more commonly affected, the facial nerve can still be damaged during a mandibular nerve block due to its proximity to the parotid gland when the needle penetrates incorrectly. This complication arises mainly through inadvertent extension past the mandibular foramen into the parotid space, leading to temporary facial paralysis reported in medical literature.

Answer choice: B
